t The Signpost platform could be used for the Parking Meter use case. Below are some Signpost-specific notes. Overview Looking at TockOS GitHub , it seems that the kernel is in Rust and the userland applications are in C. So, we could either Use the

applications are in C. So, we could either Use the Rust Host Or, use Duktape Host Some of the modules will have a Cortex M4 processor, see Duktape Eclipse Setup for how to use Duktape with the Cortex M4. The master compute module is an Intel Edison,

M4. The master compute module is an Intel Edison, see Services 16-Dec-16 call . Rust Accessor Host The Rust Accessor Host (Rusteduk) binaries are rather larger, will they fit in 256K or 512K along with the TockOS kernel? Duktape Accessor Host The Duk

h the TockOS kernel? Duktape Accessor Host The Duktape Accessor Host binaries are rather larger, will they fit in 256K or 512K along with the TockOS kernel? Hardware Hardware Summary For protoptyping, we would like a Cortex M4 with 512K of memory. Co

we would like a Cortex M4 with 512K of memory. Cortex M4 because that is what Duktape Eclipse ARM Cortex M4 512K of memory so that we have breathing room. 256K might not work. Duktape Eclipse ARM Cortex M4 was reported to have the following sizes:

rtex M4 was reported to have the following sizes: Code size = 176K Constants = 628 bytes RAM = 492 bytes That is getting close to 256K, which is why I think I need 512K None of the boards listed on the TockOS Github repo are M4 with 512K. The

ed on the TockOS Github repo are M4 with 512K. The TockOS Github repo does support the sam4l chip, and there is a M4 512K devel kit available at http://www.atmel.com/tools/ATSAM4L8-XPRO.aspx?tab=overview TockOS Boards The Tock GitHub repo boards/ dir

iew TockOS Boards The Tock GitHub repo boards/ directory contains directories for the following: Hail : Our first TockOS Accessor Board, see below imix - https://www.crowdsupply.com/helena-project/imix - ships February 28. $99 ea. Cortex M4 nrf51dk

x - ships February 28. $99 ea. Cortex M4 nrf51dk - Nordicsemi - mouser.com $39 : ARM Cortex M0 CPU and 256kB flash and 16kB RAM memory. storm - "The Firestorm is a platform from UC Berkeley's Software Defined Buildings research group. It is based o

re Defined Buildings research group. It is based on the Atmel SAM4L and includes an RF233 802.15.4 radio, a Nordic nRF51822 BLE radio, a light sensor, accelerometer and temperature sensor." http://storm.rocks/: "Storm is a 16mm x 26mm (1/2" by 1") so

rm.rocks/: "Storm is a 16mm x 26mm (1/2" by 1") solder on module that combines a 32 bit Cortex M4 microcontroller with an 802.15.4 radio, and 64 Mbit of flash memory" - unclear how to order this Hail hail "Hail is an embedded IoT module for running T

hail "Hail is an embedded IoT module for running Tock. It is programmable over USB, uses BLE for wireless, includes temperature, humidity, and light sensors, and has an onboard accelerometer. Further, it conforms to the Particle Photon form-factor."

it conforms to the Particle Photon form-factor." - Unknown availability. http://lab11.eecs.umich.edu/projects.html does not list it. Brendan has a few. See hail.pptx (account needed) for details. Hail Board The Hail Board uses a ATSAM4LC8BA , whi

Hail Board The Hail Board uses a ATSAM4LC8BA , which has 512K Flash and 64K SRAM. https://github.com/lab11/hail - Description of parts, how to commission See TockOS -> Hail for details. TockOS Chips The Tock GitHub repo chips/ directory contains: nrf

he Tock GitHub repo chips/ directory contains: nrf51 - Presumably a Nordicsemi Cortex M0 sam4l - Atmel sam4l Cortex M4 256Kb and 512Kb. Atmel SAM4L8 Xplained Pro Evaluation Kit SAM4LC8CA microcontroller: Cortex-M4 with 512KB Flash $56.30 ea. Duktape

er: Cortex-M4 with 512KB Flash $56.30 ea. Duktape Board Duktape Eclipse ARM Cortex M4 has a link to Tiva-C Microcontroller (TI) Tiva-C LaunchPad (Wikipedia) Cortex M4F (Wikipedia) has a FPU. tm4c12x development and evaluation kits dk-tm4c129x is what

evelopment and evaluation kits dk-tm4c129x is what seems to be in the video at the bottom of Duktape Eclipse ARM Cortex M4 TM4C12 9X Development Board Manual : Cortex M4: 1024-KB flash, 256KB SRAM. $199 from TI See Also Duktape Eclipse Setup - How to

99 from TI See Also Duktape Eclipse Setup - How to use Duktape with the Cortex M3 and M4 processors. Rust Host Signpost platform TockOS - Notes about how to use TockOS with the Hail Board. Edit - History - Print - Recent Changes - Search Page last mo
